title: Trad-ML pitfall drills (leakage, imbalance, calibration)
focus: The three failure modes that show up in nearly every ML interview.

items.label: Time Based Key Value Store
interviewQuestions: State your loop invariant precisely — what must be true on every iteration?
Why does the loop terminate, and how do you avoid infinite loops on the search-space update?
Walk through edge cases: empty array, target smaller than min, target larger than max, duplicates.

items.label: k-fold, stratified, leave-one-out, time-series CV
interviewQuestions: When does k-fold leak data, and what does TimeSeriesSplit do differently?
Why is stratified k-fold important for imbalanced classification?

items.label: Train/val/test split & nested CV
interviewQuestions: Why do you need both a validation and a test set for hyperparameter tuning?
What is nested cross-validation and when is it worth the cost?
How would your split strategy change for time-series forecasting vs random tabular rows?

items.label: Common forms of data leakage
interviewQuestions: Walk through three common ways data leakage sneaks into an ML pipeline.
How would you build a pipeline that prevents leakage when scaling features?

items.label: Resampling, class weights, SMOTE
interviewQuestions: Compare random oversampling, undersampling, SMOTE, and class weighting — when does each help?
Why can SMOTE leak when applied before cross-validation?

items.label: Focal loss & cost-sensitive learning
interviewQuestions: Why does focal loss help with extreme imbalance in object detection?
Compare focal loss vs class-weighted cross-entropy — when does focal actually win?

items.label: Metric choice for imbalance: PR-AUC, F-beta, MCC
interviewQuestions: Why is accuracy a terrible metric for imbalanced classification?
When do you reach for F1, F-beta, MCC, or PR-AUC?
